steps.title: Recognize the 4-term polynomial structure
steps.content: This polynomial has 4 terms. The pattern suggests factoring by grouping—we'll pair terms strategically to reveal a common factor.

steps.title: Group and factor each pair
steps.content:
Group the first two terms and the last two terms separately:
$$12x^3 + 60x^2y + xy^2 + 5y^3 = (12x^3 + 60x^2y) + (xy^2 + 5y^3)$$
Factor out the GCF from each group:
$$= 12x^2(x + 5y) + y^2(x + 5y)$$

steps.title: Extract the common binomial factor
steps.content:
Both terms now contain $(x + 5y)$. Factor it out:
$$(x + 5y)(12x^2 + y^2)$$
Reordering the factors as $(12x^2 + y^2)(x + 5y)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
